Time to First Dollar: LOW — weeks to months with active outreach
Consistency: HIGH — salary or retainer is predictable
Sales Dependence: LOW — one employer or contract client
Regime Dependence: LOW — income independent of market conditions
Fit with Strengths: HIGH — research and falsification skills directly applicable
Drift Risk: LOW — deliverables are defined by employer/client
Scale Potential: MEDIUM — capped by salary bands but can grow via reputation
Overall Verdict: STRONG FIT — highest probability near-term income path
Key Constraint: Relationship capital and willingness to apply
Notes: Fastest path to consistent income; converts existing capability directly; trade-off is autonomy ceiling

Time to First Dollar: MEDIUM — 1-3 months to first paying client with active sales
Consistency: MEDIUM — project-based income has gaps between engagements; retainer model improves consistency
Sales Dependence: HIGH — requires active client acquisition and sales process
Regime Dependence: LOW — research demand exists in both bull and bear markets
Fit with Strengths: HIGH — falsification methodology and Solana market-structure knowledge are core product
Drift Risk: HIGH — without clients to anchor work the research process becomes self-referential
Scale Potential: HIGH — can scale from solo to small team; report sales add passive layer
Overall Verdict: STRONG FIT — best long-term path if sales problem is solved
Key Constraint: Client acquisition — finding clients who value and pay for adversarial research
Notes: The research program itself is the portfolio; pre-registered designs and honest verdicts are the differentiator

Time to First Dollar: MEDIUM — 2-6 months to first retainer; requires warm introduction
Consistency: HIGH — retainer income is the most consistent structure available
Sales Dependence: MEDIUM — requires relationship capital more than cold sales; warm introductions are the primary channel
Regime Dependence: LOW — protocols need market-structure advice in all regimes
Engineering Burden: LOW — advisory work is knowledge-based not infrastructure-based
Fit with Strengths: HIGH — microstructure / liquidity / token design / risk assessment are directly applicable
Drift Risk: LOW — client deliverables anchor the work
Scale Potential: MEDIUM — limited by number of active Solana protocols with budgets; ceiling is lower than Path 2
Overall Verdict: STRONG FIT — ideal income structure; constraint is relationship capital
Key Constraint: Warm introductions to protocol founders or ecosystem funds
Notes: Best pursued in parallel with Path 2; advisory and research business reinforce each other

Time to First Dollar: HIGH — 12-24 months to meaningful subscriber revenue
Consistency: LOW to MEDIUM — subscriber churn and content treadmill create variance
Sales Dependence: MEDIUM — requires audience building rather than direct sales
Regime Dependence: LOW — honest research is valuable in all regimes
Engineering Burden: LOW — writing and publishing require minimal infrastructure
Fit with Strengths: HIGH — research output is naturally publishable; falsification angle is differentiated
Drift Risk: MEDIUM — without subscriber growth milestones the content process can drift
Scale Potential: HIGH — 500+ subscribers at $20/month is $10K/month; report sales add upside
Overall Verdict: MEDIUM FIT — strong complement to Paths 2 and 3; weak as standalone primary income
Key Constraint: Audience building — slow and uncertain; 12-24 months before income is meaningful
Notes: Best treated as distribution channel for advisory and research business rather than primary income source

Time to First Dollar: HIGH — 3-6 months to build; then uncertain time to paying customers
Consistency: MEDIUM — recurring subscription revenue once established; but churn and distribution are hard
Capital Required: LOW to MEDIUM — hosting costs but no capital at risk
Sales Dependence: MEDIUM — requires product distribution and marketing
Regime Dependence: LOW — monitoring products are valuable in all regimes
Engineering Burden: MEDIUM — production-quality monitoring system requires engineering investment and ongoing maintenance
Fit with Strengths: MEDIUM — analytical design capability exists; engineering execution is the gap
Drift Risk: MEDIUM — product development without paying customers is a drift risk
Scale Potential: HIGH — SaaS model scales well once distribution is solved
Overall Verdict: MEDIUM FIT — viable as complement to research business; weak as standalone
Key Constraint: Engineering execution and distribution — crowded market with many free alternatives
Notes: Unlock risk monitor and protocol health monitor are the highest-signal product ideas from the research program

Time to First Dollar: LOW — immediate if SOL is already held
Consistency: LOW — yield is consistent but SOL price risk dominates; net return is highly variable
Capital Required: HIGH — requires substantial SOL holdings to generate meaningful income
Regime Dependence: HIGH — net profit depends entirely on SOL price direction
Fit with Strengths: LOW — does not leverage research or falsification capability
Drift Risk: LOW — mechanical process with no research component
Scale Potential: LOW — income scales only with capital; no leverage of knowledge
Overall Verdict: POOR FIT — not a business; a leveraged SOL bet with a small yield cushion
Key Constraint: SOL price risk — 5.8% yield is irrelevant against 65% price decline
Notes: Acceptable as yield on SOL already held; not a primary income strategy

Time to First Dollar: HIGH — validator setup takes months; delegator acquisition takes years
Consistency: MEDIUM — validator commission income is consistent once established; LP income is regime-dependent
Capital Required: HIGH — validator requires hosting budget and technical investment; LP requires capital at risk
Sales Dependence: LOW for validator; LOW for LP — no client acquisition required
Regime Dependence: MEDIUM — validator income is relatively stable; LP income is regime-dependent
Engineering Burden: HIGH for validator — 24/7 uptime monitoring and technical maintenance required
Fit with Strengths: LOW — does not leverage research or falsification capability
Drift Risk: LOW — operational process with defined parameters
Scale Potential: LOW — validator income scales with delegated stake which is hard to grow; LP income scales with capital
Overall Verdict: POOR FIT — capital-intensive; technical-intensive; does not leverage demonstrated strengths
Key Constraint: Technical capability and delegator acquisition for validator; capital and regime stability for LP
Notes: Not ruled out as a supplementary income source; ruled out as a primary path given actual constraints
